title: celeri graveolens
intro: Celeri graveolens, also known as wild celery or smallage, is a biennial plant in the carrot family, characterized by its tall stalks, umbrella-like leaves, and small yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antispasmodic, diuretic, and expectorant qualities. Consuming celeri graveolens has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and aiding in the relief of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
